Fuel Efficiency and Engine Size

Author

Clara Reckhorn

How Engine Size Shapes Fuel Efficiency

Modern cars vary widely in fuel efficiency. As you scroll, we’ll explore how engine displacement relates to miles per gallon.

Step 1 — The Full Dataset

At first glance, fuel efficiency might look random across vehicles.

Each point represents a car model in the mpg dataset.

Scroll to investigate whether a pattern begins to emerge.

Step 2 — A Relationship Appears

Now focus on the overall direction of the cloud of points.

Do you notice how fuel efficiency changes as engine size increases?

Larger engines generally consume more fuel.

Step 3 — Interpreting the Trend

The downward trend suggests a negative relationship:

Higher displacement → lower MPG

Smaller engines → higher efficiency

This is a classic tradeoff between performance and efficiency.

Sticky Visualization

`geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'
